related_requirements.requirement: RF03: Accés a aplicacions des del portal
related_requirements.explanation: The Leader acts as the central routing point, deciding which functional domain (client) a user should be directed to after passing the portal layer.
It verifies the client associated with the user and routes traffic accordingly to ensure data isolation between different client domains (SEAT vs VGED).
Incorporates protection mechanisms to block unauthorized connection attempts and applies security protocols to prevent improper access.

related_requirements.requirement: RF01: Accés al portal amb autentificació
related_requirements.explanation: Provides the centralized interface and login screen where users enter credentials to access the system.
Acts as the entry point providing a unified experience where users view the list of applications they have access to.
Hosts the frontend binaries ensuring that any mention of the old name is converted to the new name across screens and data.
Ensures the presentation layer is available and updated, guaranteeing visual and functional coherence across the ecosystem consistent with the old version.

related_requirements.requirement: RF01: Accés al portal amb autentificació
related_requirements.explanation: Responsible for verifying user identity through authentication mechanisms.

related_requirements.requirement: RF04: Visualització de dades segons rols
related_requirements.explanation: Determines permissions associated with each user based on defined roles and policies, allowing or denying actions on data screens.

related_requirements.requirement: RF07: Assignar o revocar accés a aplicacions
related_requirements.explanation: Manages the creation and maintenance of roles and allows administrators to assign or revoke application access for users.
Essential for maintaining confidentiality and data protection by managing secure authentication and permission verification.
Ensures users belong to a specific Cluster and can only see brands/data contained within that Cluster, enforcing strict data separation.
Generates interactive panels representing statistics such as response times, facilitating analysis to ensure operations meet latency criteria.
Acts as an audit mechanism documenting every interaction, which aids in incident resolution and system stability monitoring.
Schedules and automates maintenance tasks (like cleaning old logs) and periodic tests to verify microservice status, ensuring continuous operation.
Uses a decoupled schema structure where each client has its own group of schemas per microservice, physically separating client data.
The database architecture enforces strict data separation per client to protect confidential information and comply with data protection regulations.
Kubernetes orchestration on AWS ensures high availability and automated deployment, scaling, and management of containers.
Allows running thousands of containers on different servers, supporting a large number of simultaneous users without performance degradation.
Implements internationalization features allowing the application to be available in multiple languages with complete translations.
As a Single-Page Application (SPA), it renders dynamic views and manages internal navigation without reloading pages, improving fluidity and response time.
Provides a dynamic and interactive user interface that keeps the user informed of procedures, contributing to ease of use.
Organizes code in layers (Presentation, Logic, Data) to process requests efficiently and optimize query performance.
Focuses on business logic, security, and data integrity on the server side, ensuring safe processing of authorized data operations.
